title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs responsive css, reflow, progressive enhancement, and performance budgets from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Mobile First Shell · changed case · Review Mobile First Shell
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 5285 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.

stimulus.title: Performance Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 7095 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A local desktop load time predicts field performance.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript. Artifact under review: responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Performance Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Performance Evidence record says responsive progressively enhanced performance budget looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Performance Evidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Performance Evidence for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Performance Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 5476 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Performance Evidence through the concrete responsive progressively enhanced performance budget case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Performance Evidence, keep Performance Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.
